jd.title: Marketing & Public Relations Business Partner

requirements.text: Bachelor’s degree in marketing, communications, journalism or related degree
At least 4 years of experience working in marketing and/or public relations
Strong writing skills with knowledge of AP style
Excellent verbal and written communication skills
Ability to develop strategic and innovative marketing and public relations strategies
Ability to build strong relationships and provide excellent client service
Experience working with professional services industry
Proficient in Microsoft Office, CRM and Hubspot
Strong project management and organizational skills
Excellent oral and written communication skills, strong interpersonal skills and the ability to clearly present complex information

responsibilities: Partner with business unit leaders to develop and execute strategic marketing and public relations plans
Develop, lead and implement promotional campaigns
Lead development of all market-specific material
Manage all marketing and public relations activities
Manage content marketing and thought leadership for owned and earned media channels
Provide project management guidance on all marketing and public relations activities and deliverables
Serve as a liaison and collaborator between business units and all department resources and teams
Manage, maintain and monitor local leads and client relationship database
jd.full_description:
Description

The Marketing & Public Relations (PR) Business Partner will develop and implement holistic strategies for Burns & McDonnell to build brand awareness, drive thought leadership and connect marketing to sales opportunities. The business partner will be an integral part of the firm’s marketing and PR department that partners with our business groups and regional offices.

The Marketing & PR Business Partner will work with 1898 & Co., a Burns & McDonnell company. 1898 & Co. is a global business, technology and security consultancy serving critical infrastructure industries. With a focus on marketing strategy and analytics, public relations, project management and creative execution, the business partner will work collaboratively to position the team’s services, projects and subject matter experts as industry leaders and ultimately position 1898 & Co., powered by the experience of Burns & McDonnell, as a top consulting partner in the industry.

The position can be based in any of the following office locations: Kansas City, Atlanta, Chicago, Denver, Houston, Minneapolis, or Wallingford. Travel to the Burns & McDonnell World Headquarters in Kansas City would be approximately once each month.

Specific responsibilities include the following, with other duties as assigned:

Partner with business unit leaders to develop and execute strategic marketing and public relations plans to generate brand awareness and client engagement among existing and targeted clients.Develop, lead and implement promotional campaigns.Lead development of all market-specific material.Manage all marketing and public relations activities including webinars, conference promotion, media opportunities and events.Manage content marketing and thought leadership for owned and earned media channels.Provide project management guidance on all marketing and public relations activities and deliverables monitoring deadlines, benchmarks and effectiveness of initiatives.Serve as a liaison and collaborator between business units and all department resources and teams, such as sales enablement and digital strategy, market insights and customer experience and creative marketing.Manage, maintain and monitor local leads and client relationship database.Review of marketing and public relations activities for consistent brand strategies.Develop trusted relationships with business and trade media contacts making the firm the go-to source for thought leadership in our industry.Manage media relations serving as a key contact for the business unit.Proactively identify opportunities to promote company projects, industry trends, corporate culture and thought leadership (and more) to targeted media outlets.Work with internal clients to research and create content for targeted media outlets via news releases, bylined articles, blogs, videos and other channels, as needed.Proactively identify and manage award opportunities to promote our people and projects.Prepare all internal clients for interviews, speaking opportunities and events by developing briefing documents with background information and talking points.Monitor and measure media coverage, developing client-facing media reports for all projects.Staff and manage interviews and media events.All other duties as assigned.

Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in marketing, communications, journalism or related degree.At least 4 years of experience working in marketing and/or public relations.Strong writing skills with knowledge of AP style.Excellent verbal and written communication skills.Experience working with professional services industry a plus.Strong media relations experience.Robust collaboration and leadership skills.Ability to develop strategic and innovative marketing and public relations strategies.Ability to build strong relationships and provide excellent client service.A self-starter with strong project management and organizational skills.Excellent oral and written communication skills, strong interpersonal skills and the ability to clearly present complex information. Proficient in Microsoft Office, CRM and Hubspot.

EEO/Minorities/Females/Disabled/Veterans

Job Public Relations

Primary Location US-MO-Kansas City

Other Locations US-MN-Minneapolis/St Paul, US-CT-Wallingford, US-CO-Denver, US-GA-Atlanta, US-TX-Houston, US-IL-Chicago

Schedule: Full-time

Travel: Yes, 5 % of the Time

Req ID: 241350

Job Hire Type Experienced #COR N/A

expected_output.summary: Results-driven professional with experience in finance, entrepreneurship, and education, skilled in Microsoft Office Suite, Venture Capital Financial Model, and fluent in Spanish. Proven track record of managing budgets, coordinating events, and mentoring students. Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with expertise in Big Data, statistical analysis, and strategy development.

experience.title: Digital M&E Research Intern
bullets: Refined quantitative and qualitative data to optimize business products for individual project managers
Evaluated Big Data to generate fundamental frameworks for normalizing product data across all entertainment brands

experience.title: Business Development Intern
bullets: Developed a five-year growth strategy for the next potential funding round of the growth firm
Generated a 50-page report that included contacting 700 colleges with an 11% response rate

experience.title: Business Development Intern
bullets: Analyzed daily and monthly partnership costs to determine day-to-day changes in cost structure
Proposed and presented 5 new key partnerships for the transition phase into TheStreet, Inc.'s Financial Content Network 2.0

experience.title: Loans & Credit Card Intern
bullets: Implemented statistical analysis of data using Excel, focusing on customer and bank databases
Completed and filed paperwork for the loan department

experience.title: Officer of Logistics and Finances
bullets: Managed and balanced the conference's $26,000+ budget
Individually raised $3,800+ for the conference from external and internal sources
Coordinated and executed a three-day conference for 390 university students throughout the US

experience.title: Officer of Finances
bullets: Administrated a budget of $800 and funding for the student organization

experience.title: Active Mentor
bullets: Volunteered to mentor and educate West Philadelphia high school students to prepare them for college

experience.title: Big Brother
bullets: Served as a positive role model and friend for children in West Philadelphia in an ongoing one-to-one relationship

experience.title: Active Mentor
bullets: Structured personal finance lesson plans to fit the needs of Philadelphia high school students

education: Bachelor of Science in Economics, The Wharton School, University of Pennsylvania, 3.45/3.06

Ability to build strong relationships and provide excellent client service

Excellent oral and written communication skills, strong interpersonal skills and the ability to clearly present complex information
